sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Team Standings
sections.text: Atalanta, sitting at an impressive 3rd place in the league with 68 points, are set to host Lecce, who are struggling in 17th position with just 27 points. This Round 34 clash at the Gewiss Stadium in Bergamo promises to be a David vs. Goliath encounter. Atalanta have been a dominant force this season, boasting the 2nd highest goals scored tally of 71, while Lecce face a tough challenge to turn their fortunes around. The disparity in league positions suggests a clear advantage for the home side. Referee Federico La Penna, known for averaging 5.1 yellow cards per game, will oversee this matchup, which could lead to a feisty affair.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these teams, Atalanta hold the edge with 3 wins compared to Lecce's 2, with no draws recorded. Their most recent encounter on August 19, 2024, saw Atalanta thrash Lecce 4-0 away from home, with Mateo Retegui earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.4 Sofascore rating. In head-to-head streaks, Atalanta have won 3 of these games without conceding in several, while Lecce have failed to keep a clean sheet in 7 straight matches against Atalanta. Over 2.5 goals have been scored in 5 of the last 7 meetings, hinting at potential for another high-scoring game.

sections.subtitle: Atalanta's Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Atalanta's attack is spearheaded by Mateo Retegui, who tops the league with 24 goals and ranks 4th in average rating at 7.36. Despite missing Ademola Lookman, who has 14 goals and ranks 3rd in the league for scoring, their depth remains strong with players like Charles De Ketelaere contributing 7 goals and ranking 3rd for big chances created with 11. Raoul Bellanova also stands out, ranking 2nd in the league for assists with 8. However, key absences like defender Berat Djimsiti, with a 69.4% ground duels won rate ranking 5th, could impact their defensive stability. Other players like Marten de Roon and Éderson will be crucial in midfield with ratings of 7.16 and 7.2 respectively.

sections.subtitle: Lecce's Struggles and Standout Performers
sections.text: Lecce are in a dire run of form, with no wins in their last 10 matches and without a clean sheet in 8 games. Their attack lacks punch, with key forward Nikola Krstović, who has 10 goals, missing this game. Defender Kialonda Gaspar offers some hope with a league-leading 78.3% ground duels won rate, while Santiago Pierotti, despite being absent, has contributed 4 goals this season. Players like Federico Baschirotto and Antonino Gallo will need to step up defensively, with ratings of 6.89 and 6.9 respectively, to contain Atalanta’s potent attack.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Styles
sections.text: Atalanta dominate in several statistical categories, ranking 2nd in total goals scored with 71, 2nd in goals conceded with just 31, and 1st in successful final third passes per game at 123.6. They also excel in penalty area entries per game, ranking 2nd with 30.7, and have created 112 big chances, also ranking 2nd in the league. Lecce, on the other hand, struggle to create opportunities, with their goal distribution showing late scoring tendencies, as 28.1% of their goals come between the 31st and 45th minutes. Atalanta’s 55.6% average ball possession, ranking 4th, contrasts with Lecce’s likely defensive approach given their current streak of conceding first in 5 of their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Atalanta have hit the woodwork 18 times this season, the most in the league, suggesting they’ve been unlucky not to score even more. They’ve also maintained less than 2.5 goals in their last 5 home matches, which might indicate a tighter game than expected. Lecce, however, have seen over 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 away games, potentially leading to set-piece opportunities. Historically, Atalanta have been the first to score in 5 of their last 7 head-to-heads with Lecce. Additionally, both teams have seen under 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 encounters, aligning with referee La Penna’s average of 5.1 yellows per game.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Tactical Insights
sections.text: The Gewiss Stadium in Bergamo has been a fortress for Atalanta, where their attacking prowess, evidenced by ranking 2nd in headers scored with 12, often overwhelms opponents. Their ability to win possessions in the final third, ranking 3rd with 4 per game, could exploit Lecce’s defensive frailties. Lecce’s streak of conceding first in 5 of their last 5 away games suggests Atalanta might take an early lead. With Atalanta averaging 5.17 corners per game, ranking 5th, and Lecce’s tendency for high corner counts away, set-pieces could play a significant role in this match.
